title: fix: parse stream/json boolean query params correctly on text routes
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Fix GET stream detection reading `request.param("stream")` (nonexistent route param) instead of the query param — streaming was never tracked on GET text routes
- Fix `z.coerce.boolean()` treating the string `"false"` as `true` — `?stream=false` / `?json=false` behaved as `true`
- One shared `parseBooleanLike` helper used by both the request schema and the track middleware; unrecognized values fail schema validation with a 400
- Unit tests for the parser and the schema

Extracted from #11567 so it can land independently — both bugs reproduce on main today.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: The truth about false
gist.blurb: Fixed a quirky bug where passing ?stream=false or ?json=false to the text API was secretly being treated as true. We've taught our query parser the difference between true and false!
gist.summary: Fixed a bug in the text API where the string 'false' in query parameters like ?stream=false or ?json=false was incorrectly evaluated as true.
gist.impact: Developers explicitly disabling streaming or JSON mode via query parameters will now get the expected behavior instead of the API ignoring their request.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Nomnom creature sorting boolean logic. Polly is organizing blocks labeled 'TRUE' and 'FALSE' while Nomnom eats a buggy piece of code. Represents fixing the boolean parsing logic.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot with a tiny tool belt sorting glowing blocks labeled 'TRUE' and 'FALSE' into correct bins, while the round tan nomnom creature happily eats a buggy piece of code. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
